findings.evidence: `rg -n "mem_support_principalDivisor_iff"` shows line 238, while adjacent names include RHS content, e.g. `mem_completeLinearSystem_iff_exists_principalDivisor` and `mem_completeLinearSystem_iff_divisorClass`.
findings.issue: `mem_support_principalDivisor_iff` is too generic for the statement `v ∈ ...support ↔ v.valuation K ... ≠ 1`; the name advertises only that some support iff exists, not the valuation-ne-one criterion.

findings.evidence: Line 313 proves `v ∈ ...support ↔ r ∈ v.asIdeal`; the grep hit `code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/Dedekind.lean:313` confirms the public name does not mention the membership target.
findings.issue: `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff` similarly omits the RHS `r ∈ v.asIdeal`, making it unclear whether the criterion is coefficient nonzero, valuation nontrivial, or ideal membership.

summary: Two new public `_iff` theorem names hide the right-hand side of the equivalence, unlike nearby TauCeti names that include the target predicate. Rename them so users can discover the valuation/membership criteria from the declaration names.
